response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer with 15+ years of experience and genuine systems-programming credentials, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Professional C++ work is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list; his recent decade is dominated by Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang web/full-stack work. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 or SDL practices, MQTT, or message-broker architectures at the depth this position demands, and there is no evidence of the people-management experience implied by a Senior Manager title.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and a track record of mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad 15+ year engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (~7 months, ended 2015); C++ absent from his own skills list
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus), or IEC 62443 / secure-development-lifecycle background
No demonstrated management or formal team-leadership experience for a Senior Manager role, plus an unexplained employment gap in 2020

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and security-standards domain fit
